channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: District Administration Imposes Section 144 in Dera Ismail Khan to Maintain Law and Order
item.description: PESHAWAR: The district administration of Dera Ismail Khan in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa has imposed Section 144 due to the current law and order situation, restricting movement on key roads and sensitive area...

item.title: IFP calls for expulsion of illegal foreign nationals in eThekwini amid immigration corruption revelations
item.description: The Inkatha Freedom Party (IFP) in eThekwini has called for stricter enforcement of South Africa's immigration laws and the removal of illegal foreign nationals from Durban. This position was expresse...

item.title: South Africans Evacuate Middle East Amid Escalating Regional Conflict
item.description: Following escalating tensions in the Middle East, South Africans are returning home after the Department of International Relations and Cooperation (DIRCO) urged citizens to evacuate. Regional airspac...

item.title: Jewish community urges coordinated security measures after multiple Toronto synagogue shootings
item.description: Toronto-area Jewish communities were targeted in three separate shootings at local synagogues within a single week, raising national concern about rising antisemitic violence. The attacks, which cause...

item.title: Oil Prices Surge Above $114 per Barrel as West Asia Conflict Escalates, Impacting Global Markets
item.description: On 9th March 2026, crude oil prices crossed $114 per barrel, marking the highest level since 2022, amid the intensifying conflict between the U.S.-Israel coalition and Iran. The war in West Asia has d...

item.title: Former Harambee Stars Coach Engin Firat Dies of Heart Attack at Istanbul Airport
item.description: Sad news has hit the football fraternity after former Harambee Stars head coach Engin Firat passed away. The Turkish tactician died on Sunday evening after suffering a sudden heart attack at Istanbul ...

item.title: Government rolls out vaccination programme to tackle FMD outbreak in Ekurhuleni
item.description: Over the weekend, Agriculture Minister John Steenhuisen and Gauteng MEC for Agriculture and Rural Development, Vuyiswa Ramokgopa, visited Ekurhuleni to lead ongoing efforts to contain Foot-and-Mouth D...

item.title: Imam, Security Guard, and Son Kidnapped by Bandits on Oke-Ode–Babanla Road in Kwara
item.description: On Sunday night, serious wahala hit residents of Ifelodun Local Government Area for Kwara State as suspected bandits abduct the Imam of Babanla, one local security guard, and the guard's pikin while d...

item.title: Greater Chennai Corporation to improve road safety with new pedestrian crossings
item.description: The Greater Chennai Corporation (GCC) has announced plans to develop pedestrian-friendly crossings at key junctions and high-footfall areas across the city. This initiative comes in response to reside...

item.title: Africa Faces Economic Risks as Strait of Hormuz Disruption Pushes Oil Prices Up
item.description: Africa dey watch situation closely as crisis for Iran fit affect global oil market. The US and Israeli strikes on Iran plus Tehran ballistic response don shut down the Strait of Hormuz, wey be main ro...

item.title: Mojtaba Khamenei appointed Iran's new Supreme Leader amid ongoing conflict with Israel and US
item.description: Iran's Assembly of Experts has appointed Mojtaba Khamenei, the 56-year-old son of the late Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, as the new Supreme Leader, just days after his father's death in Israeli airstrikes. ...

item.title: Sydney’s Parramatta Marks Holi with Colours, Dance and Tradition
item.description: In Parramatta, Sydney, the local Indian community celebrated Holi, the Hindu festival of colours, with vibrant powder, water fights, music, and dance. The festivities began in Prince Alfred Square, wh...

item.title: Alberta mountain towns prepare for wildfire season with fireguards and safety measures
item.description: As Alberta officially enters wildfire season, mountain communities such as Canmore and Hinton are bracing for potential threats, recalling the devastating 2024 Jasper fire that destroyed a third of th...

item.title: Alex Eala advances to Indian Wells round of 16 after Coco Gauff retires injured
item.description: In a round of 32 match at the 2026 Indian Wells Open in California, USA, Filipina tennis player Alex Eala progressed to the fourth round (round of 16) when her opponent, world No. 4 Coco Gauff of the ...

item.title: Escalation in Israel-Iran Conflict After Appointment of Iran's New Supreme Leader
item.description: On March 9, 2026, tensions in West Asia escalated as Iran fired its first missiles towards Israel following the appointment of Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei as the Islamic republic’s new supreme leader. ...

item.title: Starmer maintains public support despite tensions with Trump over Iran conflict
item.description: The relationship between the UK Prime Minister, Sir Keir Starmer, and US President Donald Trump has sharply deteriorated following Starmer's refusal to allow American planes to use British bases in th...

item.title: UBIC Warns Social Grant Savings Could Harm Vulnerable South Africans
item.description: The Universal Basic Income Coalition (UBIC) has voiced strong criticism of the 2026 South African Budget, arguing that government efforts to save billions on social grants could leave the most vulnera...

item.title: Bea Borres advises single mothers to prioritize financial stability before starting a family
item.description: Actress and content creator Bea Borres, who is navigating life as a single mother to her daughter Hope, recently opened up with straightforward advice for other single moms. In her shared insights, sh...

item.title: US State Department Mandates Departure of Non-Essential Diplomats and Families from Saudi Arabia Amid Escalating Regional Conflict
item.description: On March 8, 2026, the U.S. State Department issued an ordered departure directive for non-essential U.S. diplomats, government employees, and their family members stationed in Saudi Arabia, citing inc...

item.title: Sensex and Nifty May Face Decline as Global Oil Prices Exceed $105
item.description: The Indian stock market is expected to witness a decline in the coming sessions as global crude oil prices breach the $105 per barrel mark. Rising oil prices increase the cost of imports for India, wh...
